diagrams.title: Process State Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the five states of a process and transitions between them
elements: Transitions: New→Ready, Ready→Running, Running→Ready, Running→Waiting, Waiting→Ready, Running→Terminated
diagrams.purpose: Understanding process lifecycle and state transitions in OS

diagrams.title: Process Control Block Structure
diagrams.description: Data structure storing all information about a process
elements: Memory Management Info (base/limit registers, page tables)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing what information OS maintains for each process

diagrams.title: Memory Layout of Process
diagrams.description: Shows how a process's memory is organized
diagrams.purpose: Understanding process memory organization and addressing

diagrams.title: Thread vs Process Comparison
diagrams.description: Shows differences between process and thread memory sharing
elements: Process box with separate memory areas
Multiple threads sharing code, data, heap within process
Each thread having private PC, registers, stack
Process overhead vs thread lightweight nature
diagrams.purpose: Understanding thread concept and resource sharing
